key_insight: GPT-Micro combines LLM-based hypothesis generation with thermodynamics constraints and sparse data to discover compact constitutive models for manufacturing more quickly and cheaply than traditional approaches.
engineering_relevance: This could accelerate process-microstructure modeling in manufacturing while improving interpretability, physical consistency, and confidence in deployed models.
method: Extract semantic knowledge from literature with an LLM
Generate and refine constitutive model hypotheses under thermodynamics-based conservation laws
Fit and validate compact analytical models using sparse experimental data
evidence_signal: In a printed electronics testbed, the approach reportedly cut data needs by over 70% versus ML-only modeling and reduced post-data discovery time from months to hours.
watch_item: Performance depends on the quality of the available sparse data and on correctly encoding thermodynamic constraints, so errors in either can limit reliability.
paper_title: GPT-Micro: A large language paradigm for accelerated, inexpensive, and thermodynamics-consistent discovery of constitutive models in manufacturing
